title: fraxinus americana var. berlandieriana
intro: Fraxinus americana var. berlandieriana, also known as Berlandier's ash, is a deciduous tree native to the southern United States and northeastern Mexico, belonging to the Oleaceae family. The bark and leaves of Berlandier's ash have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Berlandier's ash include relief from fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Berlandier's ash can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is often used in traditional medicine to create tinctures and ointments for topical application.
